ab: Nga’ay kako a milicay tisowanan haw?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: Nga’ayto, makahi kako.
en: B: Yes, I would love to.

ab: Nga’ay kako a milicay tisowanan haw?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: Aka, caayka adihay ko kafana’an ako.
en: B:Don’t, I don’t know much about it.

ab: Ano harek no lahok ira ko romi’ad iso haw? tayra kako mihomong i tisowanan sakako nga’ay haw?
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I’d like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: Hayda, iloma’ kako a mitala i tisowanan.
en: B: Okay, I’ll wait for you at home.

ab: Ira ko romi’ad iso ano papacem haw? Sapi licaw kako i tisowanan tono iti:ayho a demak.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於阿美族的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I would like to ask you about the legends of the Amis.

ab: Talatokos kako ano papacem, caka iloma’kako.
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I’m going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won’t be home.

ab: O tengil ako adihay ko kafana’an iso tono liteng no ’Amis a demak, saka tayraan kako milicay i tisowanan.
ch: A:我聽說您對阿美族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I heard that you are very familiar with the traditional customs of the Amis, so I would like to ask you a few questions.

ab: Cowa ka adihay ko kafana’an ako. nikawrira maolah Kako a pasowal to kafana’an ako.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don’t know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: Milicay kako, nga’ay a hedoten ako a misasink haw? Nga’ay caloway a matidid.
ch: A:請問我可以錄影嗎?這樣以後比較容易記錄。
en: A: Can I record the video? It will be easier to record in the future.

ab: Hayda, mamaan saw, nga’ayto.
en: B: Okay, no problem.

ab: Pinaayto ko mihecaan iso anini ?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: Siwaayto a polo’ ira ko falo ko mihecaay kako.
en: B: I’m 98 years old.

ab: Ka^mangay ho kiso.
en: A: You’re so young.

ab: Icowasaw, malitengayto kako.
en: B: No, I’m not! I’m old.

ab: O nani cowaay kiso?
en: A: Where are you from?

ab: O nani etolan nanay kako.
en: B: I’m from Turan.

ab: O ’Amis ko fafahi iso haw?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是阿美族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also Ami?

ab: Cowa, o Piwma cingra.
en: B:No, she’s a Puyuma.

ab: Hakowaay ko kafana’an iso a caciyaw?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: Mafana’ay kako to sowal no ’Amis ato no Piwma.
ch: B: 我會說阿美語和卑南語。
en: B: I speak Aramaic and Puyuma.

ab: anini a romi’ad mahemek kako a milicay i tisowanan, pasowalen haca kako to caay kang’ay a demaken ano mi’adop.
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It’s a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ab: Hayda, yo kapahho kako mararid kako a tala tokos a mi’adop.
ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: Ahowiday, anini a romi’ad a dihay ko pakafana’ iso tadanga’ay.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: Oso’elinay kona misowalan ako. o sowal no malitengay konini.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: Hatini aca ko kalalicay ita anini a romi’ad. ahowiday to pakafana’ iso.
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: That’s all for today’s interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: Aka ngodo, liyaw sato kita a malicay.
en: B: You’re welcome, let’s talk again.

ab: Miliyas to kako, nanay ano miliyaw adihay ko matengilay a tawciker no liteng.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到更多的故事。
en: A: I’m going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: Hayi, cedengen ko rakat.
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
